title: Design of Algorithms & Theory of Computation (JNTUK R20)
description: JNTUK R20 Year 3 — Design and Analysis of Algorithms (DAA) covering complexity, greedy, DP, backtracking; Theory of Computation (TOC/FLAT) covering automata, grammars, and computability. Aligned to JNTUK B.Tech CSE R20 syllabus.

modules.title: Getting Started
modules.description: Introduction and overview of Design of Algorithms & Theory of Computation (JNTUK R20)

pages.title: Algorithms & TOC — Course Overview
Algorithms & TOC — Wiki Index

modules.title: Core Lectures
modules.description: All lectures and concept pages for this course

pages.title: Algorithm Analysis: Asymptotic Notation, Recurrences, Master Theorem
Divide and Conquer: Merge Sort, Quick Sort, Binary Search, Strassen's Matrix
Greedy Algorithms: Activity Selection, Huffman Coding, Kruskal, Prim
Dynamic Programming: Memoization, Tabulation, LCS, Knapsack, Matrix Chain
Backtracking: N-Queens, Graph Coloring, Subset Sum, Sudoku Solver
Graph Algorithms: BFS, DFS, Dijkstra, Bellman-Ford, Floyd-Warshall, Topological Sort
NP-Completeness: P vs NP, NP-Hard, NP-Complete Problems, Cook's Theorem
Introduction to Automata: DFA, NFA, Equivalence, ε-NFA and Closure Properties
Regular Languages: Regular Expressions, Pumping Lemma, Minimization of DFA
Context-Free Grammars: Derivations, Parse Trees, Ambiguity, CNF, GNF
Pushdown Automata: Definition, DPDA vs NPDA, CFL Closure Properties
Turing Machines: Definition, Variants, Decidability, Halting Problem, Undecidability

modules.title: Practice
modules.description: Practice, quizzes, coding, and project pages

pages.title: DAA Lab: Implementation of Greedy, DP, and Backtracking Algorithms in C++/Java
Placement Guide: Algorithms and TOC Interview Questions — JNTUK Pattern
